insight: Xaetron works best when paired with another defender that has click ability, such as Omega Splitter or Arka, allowing you to alternate between defenders and maximize absorb efficiency.
author: totally_not_fbi, amalloy
context: Discussion of optimal Xaetron usage patterns in defense.

insight: Endotherm Kit is not a unit to rush or build for proactively; rather, it is an option used to check specific opponent strategies in slow, single-absorber sets, and performs poorly in fast games where opponents can simply buy damage.
author: consensus (swapgo, crash_overlord)
context: Evaluation of Endotherm Kit's role and when it is effective.

insight: Iso Kronus is a losing proposition against Xaetron because Xaetron can heal 8 HP every 2 turns, making it function like a 9 HP absorber against pure Iso attacks.
author: swapgo, supergreyman
context: Analysis of matchup between Iso Kronus and Xaetron defenses.

insight: In heavy green sets with Vivid Drone, P1 can execute DD DDE D1C opening as a viable curve option.
author: mrguy888
context: First-turn opening selection in green-heavy unit sets.

insight: When stabilized with a drone advantage, you must also be ahead on swords (attack) to win, as supplies matter; being up on drones but behind on attack is insufficient.
author: leary93
context: Clarification on what stabilization actually means in game evaluation.

insight: Immolite is approximately the same efficiency as Tarsier, but performs worse as a first attacker since it cannot efficiently ramp attack against the first defensive unit.
author: leary93, Deleted User
context: Efficiency comparison of red attackers in early game.

insight: P2 T1 DC (Drone-Conduit) into Gauss Cannons can work well but requires careful play; building too many Forcefields to keep drones alive may slow the cannon rush.
author: amalloy, Deleted User
context: Evaluation of P2 cannon rush openers in base sets.

insight: In cannon rush sets with Thorium Dynamo available, converting drones to Dynamos is preferable to building many Engineers, as Dynamos provide both offense and economy.
author: amalloy
context: Unit prioritization in cannon-dynamo sets.

insight: Calculating freeze threats effectively requires adding chill to attack, then subtracting 'fake chill' (non-granular chills where defenders have less health than the chill value), rather than just adding attack and chill.
author: consensus (namington, totally_not_fbi, cogito3)
context: Strategies for mentally calculating freeze threats during gameplay.

insight: Arka is often the most important unit in a set because the first Arka purchase enables double Rager follow-up; prioritizing Arka acquisition (reaching 7 gold ASAP) is usually correct over building threats that don't immediately synergize.
author: mrguy888
context: Replay analysis of set prioritization with Arka present.

insight: When a powerful unit like Arka delays a turn of double Rager (denying 6 absorb) or delays a critical unit like Defense Grid or Centurion, you should purchase the power units immediately rather than delay further.
author: mrguy888
context: Decision-making on when to commit to high-value unit purchases.

insight: Zemora Voidbringer is playable but requires specific conditions: a certain environment, significant investment, and is not an auto-win even when it begins firing.
author: miuna6933
context: Assessment of Zemora's viability and requirements.

insight: Galvani Drone is an economical add-on only when you can afford it without exploitation risk and while still having resources to spend; buying Galvanis without these constraints leaves you vulnerable.
author: darkeisbein, xujhan
context: Valuation and conditions for building alternate drones.

insight: Engineer + Galvani Drone combination is roughly equal to a standard Drone in efficiency, but Galvani + Antima Comet is exceptionally strong ('disgusting'), and the winner is often decided by the first Antima in such sets.
author: xujhan
context: Specific synergy evaluation between alternate drones and powerful units.

insight: Typically 2 Galvani Drones is the standard number if you can replace your Engineers every turn; more are good if playing against threat or with units that generate Engineers like Hellhound, Tesla, or Antima Comet.
author: xyotsuba
context: Quantitative guidance on Galvani purchases in standard games.

insight: Shredder is very efficient as soak (like Infusion Grid) and only becomes inefficient if it forces you to not achieve full absorb; when used as a clicked attacker that dies immediately while soaking 4 HP, it represents strong value.
author: namington, supergreyman
context: Valuation of Shredder in different roles.

insight: Shredder is one of the most breach-proof non-green attackers and works exceptionally well in Dynamo sets because you can absorb while convenient and avoid wasting blue after it becomes inconvenient; holding Shredder can also save Dynamos by forcing opponents to kill damage first.
author: mrguy888, xyotsuba
context: Strategic flexibility and value of Shredder in specific set types.

insight: Militia is an attacker that still gets value against absorb (at least more than half value as it forces the opponent to build Wall), and can function as a first attacker that doesn't get fully absorbed; it is context-dependent whether it is superior to Shredder.
author: mrguy888
context: Comparative analysis of alternative attackers.

insight: Frontline units (like Hannibull) are good when you can soak with them while still maintaining full absorb value, but are often exploited if bought when the opponent already has sufficient attack to kill them immediately.
author: amalloy
context: Evaluation of when to purchase high-health defensive units.

insight: Feral Warden functions as a Wall on the turn you buy it (providing absorb), then as a Tarsier (attacking attacker) for the remainder of the game, making it exceptionally efficient.
author: cactus_wren
context: Discovery and evaluation of unit synergies through gameplay.

insight: One Engineer in a DDE opening is worth the cost if it results in 2-3 additional drones, since the Engineer still functions as a blocker and one extra drone per turn is valuable.
author: amalloy, supergreyman
context: Cost-benefit analysis of early Engineer purchases.

insight: Cynestra is expensive (requires 12GGR to purchase in a single turn) and not all sets allow efficient spending of this cost; however, when you cannot out-greed a Cynestra player, you must either go under (rush) or mirror their Cynestras.
author: cactus_wren, mrguy888, amalloy
context: Strategic options when facing Cynestra-focused strategies.

insight: Odin does not gain significant value in sets with Energy Matrix, as Matrix negates much of Odin's absorb value; Tarsier spam behind Matrix is often preferable.
author: supergreyman, cactus_wren
context: Unit synergy evaluation in specific sets.

insight: Brooder is a strong counter to Plexo Cell because it forces Plexo Drones into short lifespan, making multiple Plexo Drones simultaneously create cascading inefficiency.
author: mrguy888, xyotsuba
context: Analysis of unit interactions in freeze-heavy sets.

insight: Scorchilla's main value is against Colossus due to exceptional hold value, but is otherwise outclassed by other attackers in most sets.
author: xyotsuba, amalloy
context: Niche application evaluation for specialized unit.

insight: Wild Drone is only sub-optimal if the opponent can easily reach 3 attack (e.g., Tesla Coil in set), and even then at least 1 Wild Drone is usually correct for the gold discount value.
author: cogito3, namington
context: Valuation of alternate drone in different set environments.

insight: In Arms Race mirror matchups, Arms Race itself is already acceptable as a first attacker, and adding Tarsier on top allows follow-up with 1-2 attack next turn while forcing defense at equivalent timing to buying 2 attack normally.
author: leary93
context: Evaluation of Arms Race efficiency in aggressive economic mirrors.
